Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>為何聽牌且在補牌而且該手結束有消除時不會先補完牌才消?</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-06-29</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
全部確認。Scatter=19、SilverScatter=20、COL=6、ROW=5、`pos = col*5 + row`。根因鎖定。

## 根因：NearWin 補牌迴圈的 `else break` 把其他列的天降丟掉了

**問題在 `SlotReels.ts:3780-3782`**，NearWin 逐列天降迴圈的收尾：

```js
for (let col = 0; col < Game_Define.COL; col++) {
    const entries = colMap.get(col);
    if (!entries || entries.length === 0) continue;
    this.m_gameView.EffectPlate.ShowNearWin(col);
    this.PlayNearWinSound();
    for (const entry of entries) {
        await this.EnterPlate(...);          // ← 實際天降
        if (entry.isScatter) {
            this.m_scatterCollectCount++;
            if (this.m_scatterCollectCount >= SCATTER_NEARWIN_MAX_COUNT) {  // 3765：滿 5 顆
                this.m_isHasScatterNearWin = false;   // ← NearWin 關掉
                ...
            }
        }
    }
    if (this.m_isHasScatterNearWin) {
        this.m_gameView.EffectPlate.StopNearWinAt(col);
        await Define.Wait(this, delay);
    } else {
        break;          // ← BUG：NearWin 一關，整個 col 迴圈直接中斷
    }
}
```

`break` 一跳出，`colMap` 裡**還沒處理的 col 就再也不會呼叫 `EnterPlate`**，那幾列的天降符號永遠沒掉進來。接著方法往下走完 → MATCHING_PATCH_UP 結束 → COUNT_MULT → 直接 EXPLODE 消除下一盤。這就是你看到的「掉完 col 0 就跳去消除」。

## 用你那手資料對證（已確認，非推測）

- 進這手時 `m_scatterCollectCount`≈4（plate[0] 盤面有 4 顆 Scatter：col2/3/4/5 的 19），NearWin 觸發中。
- plate[0] 消除的空位：col0(2格)、col1(1格)、col4(3格)、col5(2格)。
- 補牌來源是 plate[1]。**col0 的填充取 plate[1] col0 頂端 = `[6, 19]`**（`MatchingPatchUpState.ts:108`），含一顆 Scatter(19)。
- 迴圈先跑 col0 → 掉到那顆 19 → `m_scatterCollectCount` 4→**5** → 命中 `>= SCATTER_NEARWIN_MAX_COUNT`(3765) → `m_isHasScatterNearWin=false` → 收尾走 `else break`。
- **結果 col1、col4、col5 的天降被整個略過**，盤面缺符號就進了消除。完全符合你的描述。

設計原意應該是「Scatter 滿 5 顆後停掉 NearWin 的逐顆慢放節奏」，但它**連帶把剩下該掉的牌也一起 break 掉了**——停的應該是「節奏特效」，不是「補牌本身」。

## 修法方向（需你確認後我再動）

核心：NearWin 中途關掉時，**剩餘列仍要補完，只是不要再走 NearWin 的慢放/等待**。把 `else break` 拿掉，並把 `ShowNearWin`/`PlayNearWinSound`/`StopNearWinAt`/`Wait` 用 `if (m_isHasScatterNearWin)` 包住。兩個落地節奏選擇：

- **A（最小改動）**：剩餘列維持原本逐顆 `await EnterPlate`，只是省掉列間 `Wait`。最 surgical。
- **B（視覺較順）**：NearWin 一關，剩餘列的 `pendingFills` 改走並行天降（同 3786 非 NearWin 分支），落地感跟一般補牌一致。

我傾向 **A**（改動面最小、風險低；剩 3 列逐顆掉幾乎無感），但 B 視覺更接近「正常補完」。

---

兩個確認：
1. 這個根因和修法方向你認同嗎？
2. 要 A 還是 B？

你點頭後我會先補因果鏈分析（會掃 `m_isHasScatterNearWin` / `m_scatterCollectCount` 其他使用點，確認改 break 不影響跨 cascade 的 NearWin 持續邏輯）再動 `SlotReels.ts`。

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). The user is asking me to generate a progress summary of what was done in the primary Claude session. Let me parse what happened:

The user asked (in Chinese): "為何聽牌且在補牌而且該手結束有消除時不會先補完牌才消?" which translates to something like "Why doesn't it complete filling the tiles before eliminating when near-win is active and tiles are being filled and there's elimination at the end of that hand?"

Claude's response identified a root cause in the code:
- The issue is in `SlotReels.ts:3780-3782` in the NearWin tile-filling loop
- There's an `else break` that terminates the column loop prematurely
- When scatter count reaches 5 (`m_scatterCollectCount >= SCATTER_NEARWIN_MAX_COUNT`), the NearWin flag is disabled and the loop breaks
- This causes remaining columns to skip their tile drops entirely
- The specific case: col0 drops a scatter making count 4→5, triggers the break, and col1, col4, col5 never get their tiles filled
- The game then proceeds to elimination with missing symbols

Claude proposed two fix approaches:
- A: Minimal change - keep sequential drops but remove the break and wrap special effects in conditional checks
- B: Visual approach - switch remaining columns to parallel drops like normal filling

The work is in investigation/diagnosis phase - no code has been modified yet. Claude is waiting for user confirmation on the root cause analysis and which fix approach to take before proceeding.

content.text:
<summary>
  <request>Debug why tiles aren't filled completely before elimination when NearWin is active and scatter threshold is reached during tile filling</request>
  
  <investigated>The NearWin tile-filling logic in `SlotReels.ts` lines 3780-3782 was examined. The code loops through columns to drop tiles with NearWin effects, and tracks scatter count. When the 5th scatter drops (reaching `SCATTER_NEARWIN_MAX_COUNT`), `m_isHasScatterNearWin` is set to false and an `else break` statement terminates the column loop. The specific case was traced: col0 drops a scatter (count 4→5), triggers the threshold, and the break statement prevents col1, col4, and col5 from receiving their pending tile drops.</investigated>
  
  <learned>The `else break` at line 3782 in `SlotReels.ts` exits the entire column loop when NearWin is disabled mid-process. This causes any columns after the one that triggered the scatter threshold to skip their `EnterPlate()` calls entirely. The remaining tiles in `colMap` are never processed, leaving gaps in the board that proceed directly to elimination state. The original intent was to stop the slow NearWin pacing effects after 5 scatters, but the implementation also stops the tile filling itself.</learned>
  
  <completed>Root cause identified and confirmed with actual game data. The bug is definitively located in the NearWin column loop's early exit logic in `SlotReels.ts`.</completed>
  
  <next_steps>Awaiting user confirmation on the root cause analysis and selection between two fix approaches: (A) minimal change keeping sequential drops but removing the break and conditionalizing effects, or (B) switching remaining columns to parallel drops for visual consistency. Once approved, will analyze impact on `m_isHasScatterNearWin` and `m_scatterCollectCount` usage across cascades before implementing the fix.</next_steps>
  
  <notes>The bug only manifests when the 5th scatter drops during NearWin tile filling with multiple columns pending. The break statement conflates "stop NearWin effects" with "stop filling tiles entirely" - the fix needs to separate these two concerns.</notes>
</summary>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>NearWin tile-filling logic structure examined before fix</title>
  <subtitle>Reviewed SlotReels.ts NearWin column loop showing scatter threshold handling and problematic early break</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>SlotReels.ts lines 3742-3786 contain the NearWin tile-filling logic with column-by-column sequential drops</fact>
    <fact>Line 3765 checks if m_scatterCollectCount >= SCATTER_NEARWIN_MAX_COUNT and sets m_isHasScatterNearWin to false</fact>
    <fact>Lines 3774-3782 contain the post-column logic with m_isHasScatterNearWin check followed by else break statement</fact>
    <fact>The break statement at line 3781 terminates the entire column loop when NearWin is disabled mid-process</fact>
    <fact>Non-NearWin path at line 3784 uses parallel tile drops via fillPromises array</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The exact code structure of the NearWin tile-filling bug was examined in SlotReels.ts. The logic groups pendingFills by column into a colMap, then iterates through each column (0 to COL-1) sequentially. For each column, it shows NearWin effects, plays sound, then awaits EnterPlate for each tile in that column. When a scatter drops, m_scatterCollectCount increments, and if it reaches SCATTER_NEARWIN_MAX_COUNT (5), the flag m_isHasScatterNearWin is set to false and NearWin effects stop. The critical bug is in the post-column check: if m_isHasScatterNearWin is still true, it stops NearWin effects for that column and waits; else it breaks out of the entire column loop, abandoning any remaining columns that haven't been processed yet. This causes unprocessed columns to never receive their tile drops.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>

};\\n        tempoSettingCb();\\n        tempoSetting?.AddCb( tempoSettingCb );\\n\\n        this.CheckTestKeyCode();\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 初始化\\n     * @param gameView 遊戲視窗\\n     */\\n    Init( gameView: GameView )\\n    {\\n        this.m_gameView = gameView;\\n        this.InitValue();\\n    }\\n\\n    /**@ch 設定初始盤面 */\\n    CreateSymbol()\\n    {\\n        // plate = 0 下，plate = 1    this.m_isHasScatterNearWin = false;\\n\\n        // 強制關閉上一局殘留的 NearWin 特效\\n        if (this.m_scatterNearWinColumn > -1) {\\n            this.m_scatterNearWinColumn = -1;\\n            this.StopNearWinSound();\\n            this.SetSymbolDark(false, -1);\\n        }\\n        this.m_gameView.EffectPlate.StopNearWin();\\n\\n        // 恢復上一局隱藏的 Scatter symbol\\n        for (const pos this.m_isChangeToFinalSymbol[ col ] = false;\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        // 清空並重新初始化有效的列索引（只對中輪帶）\\n        this.m_isNotChangeSymbolThisRound = {};\\n        for ( let col = 0; col < this.m_upDownReelChangeState[ nodeName ] = true;\\n                }\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        // 設定假轉輪帶隨機行數位置\\n\\n        // 掉落模式：「按 Spin（spin 開始）即觸發舊盤面掉出」——在此 StartSpin 末端觸發，\\n 觸發「舊盤面掉出畫面」（由 StartSpin 末端在「按 Spin（spin 開始）」時點呼叫）\\n     * @description INV-1：同步成對設定凍結旗標 + 建立動畫 promise，無中間 await。\\n     *              INV-4：對「已在 drop mode」為 no-op（冪等），防止同一局重複觸發建立孤兒 promise。\\n     */\\n    public TriggerDropOut(): void {\\n        // INV-4 冪等：冪等鍵改判「動畫 handle 是否已建立」（非凍結旗標 m_isInDropMode）。\\n        // 因 m_isInDropMode 自 StartSpin 起即為 true（凍結），不能再當冪等鍵，否則 promise 永不建立（FP-1）。\\n Promise<void> {\\n        if (this.m_dropAllSymbolsOutOfScreenPromise) {\\n            await this.m_dropAllSymbolsOutOfScreenPromise;\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 將所有輪帶的 Symbol content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Conditionalized NearWin effects in tile-filling loop (step 1 of fix)</title>
  <subtitle>Wrapped ShowNearWin and PlayNearWinSound calls with m_isHasScatterNearWin check to decouple effects from tile filling</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>SlotReels.ts lines 3756-3759 now wrap ShowNearWin and PlayNearWinSound in if(m_isHasScatterNearWin) conditional</fact>
    <fact>The tile filling for-loop continues regardless of m_isHasScatterNearWin state after this change</fact>
    <fact>This is step 1 of the minimal-change fix approach A to resolve the early break bug</fact>
    <fact>Comment added: "NearWin 仍開著才顯示該列 tease 光效/音效；關掉後仍要補完剩餘列,只是不再 tease"</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The first step of the NearWin tile-filling bug fix was implemented by conditionalizing the visual and audio tease effects. Previously, ShowNearWin and PlayNearWinSound were called unconditionally at the start of each column's tile drop sequence. Now these calls are wrapped in an if(m_isHasScatterNearWin) check, so when the scatter threshold is reached mid-loop and the flag is set to false, subsequent columns skip the NearWin tease effects but continue with tile filling. This decouples the "stop NearWin effects" behavior from "stop filling tiles" behavior, addressing the root cause where visual effect control was incorrectly terminating essential game logic. The tile-filling loop itself continues to execute for all columns regardless of the flag state.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/SlotReels.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.thinking:
This is the second step of the fix. The user removed the `else break` statement that was causing columns to skip tile filling, and added a comment explaining why the break must not be there. The fix is now complete according to the minimal-change approach (A).

Key changes:
1. Removed the `else { break; }` block at lines 3781-3782
2. Added a comment explaining that when NearWin is disabled (after 5th scatter), the pacing effects should stop but tile filling must continue
3. The column loop now continues to process all columns regardless of m_isHasScatterNearWin state

This completes the bug fix by ensuring that when the scatter threshold is reached mid-loop, the remaining columns still get their tiles filled, just without the NearWin visual and audio tease effects.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Removed early break statement to complete NearWin tile-filling bug fix</title>
  <subtitle>Deleted else break block at line 3781 that was terminating column loop prematurely after scatter threshold</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>SlotReels.ts lines 3781-3782 else break statement completely removed from NearWin column loop</fact>
    <fact>Added comment explaining that slow-pacing rhythm should stop but tile filling must continue when NearWin is disabled</fact>
    <fact>Column loop now processes all remaining columns after scatter threshold is reached instead of terminating early</fact>
    <fact>This completes the minimal-change fix approach A selected earlier to resolve tile-filling bug</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The second and final step of the NearWin tile-filling bug fix was implemented by removing the problematic else break statement. Previously, when the 5th scatter dropped and m_isHasScatterNearWin was set to false, the else branch would execute a break statement that terminated the entire column loop, causing any remaining columns to never receive their tile drops. The fix removes this else break block entirely, so the loop continues to process all columns in the colMap regardless of the NearWin flag state. A comment was added explaining that when NearWin is disabled after reaching 5 scatters, only the slow-pacing rhythm (StopNearWinAt and Wait calls) should stop, but tile filling itself must complete for all remaining columns to prevent board state errors. This surgical change preserves all existing tile drop behavior while fixing the root cause that was conflating "stop NearWin effects" with "stop tile filling logic".</narrative>
